title: Spinach & Cheese Muffins
steps: Preheat the oven to 180C.
Melt the butter in a large bowl.
Add the eggs and whisk.
Add the milk and mix.
Defrost the spinach in a small bowl in the microwave, then chop it.
Grate the cheese.
Sieve the plain flour into the large bowl with the butter, eggs and milk.
Add the cheese and chopped spinach, then mix.
Grease a muffin tray with butter.
Spoon the mixture into the tray and bake for 15-16 minutes until golden.
notes: Toddler-friendly snack. Keep as recipe-only by default rather than adding to the weekly meal list.

title: Coconut Chicken Curry

ingredients.name: chicken breasts
ingredients.notes: skinless and boneless, cut into bite-size pieces

steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan or shallow casserole, add the chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken for about 5 minutes or until no longer pink.
Add the onion, garlic and curry powder, stir and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add the chicken stock, coconut milk, chopped tomatoes, tomato puree and sugar, then stir everything together.
Bring to the boil, cover with a lid, turn the heat down and simmer for 15–20 minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve over rice.
notes: Scaled to serve 4 (UK family). Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days, or freeze once fully cooled. Curry powder blends vary in heat — start with 1 tbsp if unsure. Mild curry powder works best for a family-friendly version.

title: Classic Omurice (Japanese chicken rice omelette)

source.label: Sirogohan.com — 基本のオムライス

ingredients.name: Chicken Rice
ingredients.notes: see the Chicken Rice recipe in this cookbook — make it first, divide into 4 portions, keep warm

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: 2 per portion, cook one portion at a time

steps: Make a batch of Chicken Rice (see the Chicken Rice recipe). Keep warm and divide into 4 portions.
For each portion: crack 2 eggs into a bowl, add 2 pinches of salt, and beat hard with chopsticks, dragging along the base of the bowl until no streaks of white remain.
Melt a knob of butter in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat. Pour in the egg mixture and stir gently as it sets so the surface stays half-cooked.
Place one portion of chicken rice in the centre of the egg, fold the sides over, then tip the pan to roll it onto a plate seam-side down.
Repeat for the remaining three servings.
Make the ketchup-butter sauce: combine 8 tbsp ketchup, 3 tbsp water and ~20g butter in a small pan over low heat, stirring until the butter melts and the sauce is glossy.
Spoon the sauce over each omurice and serve immediately.
notes: Built on top of the Chicken Rice recipe — make that first, then this becomes a quick assembly. The ketchup-butter sauce is the signature finishing touch.

title: Smoky & Creamy Caramelised Onion Butter Beans
steps: In a medium frying pan over medium heat, add a couple of tbsp of oil then the onions. Fry until softened and caramelised, stirring often.
Add the garlic and tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Deglaze the pan with the white wine and simmer for a couple of minutes until mostly evaporated. Skip if no wine to hand.
Add the butter beans with their liquid and the lentils.
Add the double cream and bring to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Squeeze in the lemon juice and taste for seasoning.
Top with chopped parsley and basil if using, and serve.
notes: Laura's tweaks applied: 2 cans of butter beans, lentils added, tomato purée and cream doubled. Lentils added with the beans — if using dried lentils rather than pre-cooked, add extra liquid and simmer longer until tender.

title: Japanese Curry

ingredients.name: meat (beef, chicken, pork or prawns)
ingredients.notes: brown first; skip for a vegetarian version

ingredients.name: garam masala
ingredients.notes: plus optional extra 1 tsp at the end

ingredients.name: Japanese curry cubes
ingredients.notes: e.g. S&B Golden Curry — choose mild for kid-friendly

ingredients.name: milk or extra water
ingredients.notes: to loosen if the sauce is too thick

steps: Dice the onion. In a large pan or pot, brown your chosen meat (beef, chicken, pork or prawns) with the onion. Skip the meat for a vegetarian version.
Add enough water to cover, then add the carrots and potatoes.
Add a handful of fresh tomatoes (or 1/4 tin of chopped tomatoes) and stir in 1 tsp sugar if using.
Season with salt, pepper and 1 tbsp garam masala.
Simmer with meat for 1–2 hours; for vegetable-only, simmer up to 45 minutes.
Lower the heat. Drop in the Japanese curry cubes and stir until they dissolve and the sauce is smooth.
If the sauce is too thick, loosen with a splash of milk or water.
Optionally add another 1 tsp of garam masala at the end for a stronger spice hit.
Optional: serve alongside katsu chicken, pork or prawns.
Serve over rice or udon noodles.
notes: Family recipe. Flexible — works with any protein or veg-only. Japanese curry cubes (S&B Golden Curry, House Vermont Curry, etc.) are the key flavour base; choose mild for the toddler-friendly version. Long meat simmer (1–2h) makes the meat tender; reduce to 45 minutes for veg-only.

title: Miso Soup

ingredients.name: dark miso paste
ingredients.notes: to taste — add residue at the bottom for flavour

steps: Bring water to the boil in a saucepan.
Add the dashi tea bag and let it infuse, then remove it once the water has boiled.
Stir in the dashi powder.
Add the white onion, potato and carrot. Simmer until just tender.
Add the mangetout and cook briefly.
Once everything is cooked through, turn off the heat.
Add the tofu (cubed) and wakame seaweed.
Stir in the dark miso paste — about 1 tbsp to taste — including any residue from the bottom of the tub for extra flavour.
Stir in the light miso paste — another 1 tbsp or so to taste.
Warm gently to serve. Don't boil after adding the miso — it dulls the flavour.
notes: Family recipe. Keeps well in the fridge for up to 3 days; reheat gently rather than boiling.

title: Yorkshire Puddings

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: a little, for the muffin tin

steps: Whisk the flour, eggs and milk together into a smooth batter.
Rest the batter in the fridge before cooking — it helps them rise.
Add a little oil to each cup of a muffin tin and preheat in a hot oven (220°C / 200°C fan) until the oil is smoking hot.
Pour the batter into the hot oil — equal amounts per cup.
Return the tin straight back to the oven. Try not to open the door once they're in — they'll fail to rise.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until risen and golden.
notes: Family recipe. Side dish — makes 6 large Yorkshire puddings (plenty for a family of 4 with leftovers). The cold batter + smoking-hot oil contrast is what gives them the rise.

title: Pancakes / Crepes
steps: Whisk flour, eggs and milk together into a smooth, thin batter.
Heat a medium pan to medium-hot, lightly greased.
Ladle the batter into the pan, swirling to coat the base in a thin layer.
Fry for 30–60 seconds per side until lightly golden.
notes: Family recipe — thin French-style crepes. Sweet (lemon and sugar, Nutella) or savoury (cheese and ham). Makes 6–8 crepes.

title: Chilli con carne

ingredients.name: chilli spice mix
ingredients.notes: chilli powder, cumin, paprika to taste

steps: Fry the onion for 5 minutes until softening.
Add the pepper and spices and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add the beef mince and brown for 5–10 minutes.
Pour in the stock, chopped tomatoes and tomato puree, season well and stir.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes with the lid on.
Stir in the kidney beans and cook for a further 10 minutes with the lid off.
notes: Family recipe. Freezer-friendly. Pairs with rice or jacket potatoes; for kid-friendly, dial back the chilli powder.

title: Chicken Rice (チキンライス)

ingredients.name: tomato ketchup
ingredients.notes: use 3.5 tbsp if you skip the tomato puree

ingredients.name: tomato puree
ingredients.notes: 3× concentrated; halve if using tomato paste; optional but adds depth

ingredients.name: salted butter
ingredients.notes: unsalted is fine — adjust salt at the end

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: to taste, added at the finish

steps: Dice the chicken thigh into ~1cm cubes, the carrot into 4–5mm pieces, and the onion into 5–7mm pieces.
Melt about half the butter (~7g) in a frying pan over medium heat. Add the chicken and cook for 2–3 minutes until no longer pink.
Add the onion and carrot. Stir-fry for 3–4 minutes until the onion turns translucent and the carrot starts to soften.
Push the ingredients to one side of the pan and add the ketchup and tomato puree to the empty space. Let them bubble for a moment to take the raw edge off.
Add the warm rice and stir-fry to combine, breaking up any clumps until everything is evenly coloured.
Stir in the remaining butter, season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve.
notes: Japanese yōshoku classic — also the base for our Omurice recipe (wrap a portion in a folded omelette and top with ketchup-butter sauce). Keeps ~3 days in the fridge or ~1 month frozen.

title: Easy Yoghurt Flatbreads
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder and salt in a bowl, then stir in the yoghurt with a wooden spoon until it forms a rough dough.
Tip onto a lightly floured surface and knead briefly until smooth, adding a little more flour if sticky.
Divide into 4 equal balls and roll each out to roughly the size of a saucer, about 3–4mm thick.
Heat a dry frying pan (or lightly oiled) over a medium-high heat. Cook each flatbread for 1–2 minutes per side until puffed and charred in spots.
Stack and wrap in a clean tea towel to keep warm and soft while you cook the rest.
notes: Toddler-friendly side. Quantities make 4 dinner-plate rounds — divide into 6 medium or 8 small if you'd rather. Optional: brush with melted garlic butter and chopped coriander after cooking. [ASSUMPTION] Quantities follow the standard BBC Good Food / UK 2-ingredient yoghurt flatbread formula (equal weights flour + yoghurt) — the source page wasn't directly fetchable; verify before relying on it.

title: Shakshuka
steps: Heat the olive oil in a wide, lidded frying pan over medium heat. Add the onion and pepper and cook for 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, paprika, cumin and chilli flakes. Cook for 1 minute, stirring.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and add the sugar. Season well, stir and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Make 4 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover with the lid and cook over low heat for 5–8 minutes, until the whites are just set but the yolks are still runny (or cook longer if you prefer firm yolks).
Scatter over crumbled feta and fresh herbs. Serve straight from the pan with flatbreads or crusty bread.
notes: Quick one-pan weeknight meal. Skip the chilli flakes for a toddler-friendly version. Feta is optional but adds a salty, creamy contrast. Leftovers keep in the fridge for 1 day — reheat gently in a pan.

title: Red Lentil Dhal

ingredients.name: spinach
ingredients.notes: optional, stirred in at the end

steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 8 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Add the garlic, ginger, cumin, turmeric, garam masala and chilli powder (if using). Cook for 1–2 minutes, stirring.
Add the lentils and stir to coat in the spices.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes, coconut milk and water or stock. Stir and bring to the boil.
Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are completely soft and the dhal has thickened.
Stir in the spinach (if using) and let it wilt for 1–2 minutes.
Season with salt, add an extra pinch of garam masala to taste, and serve with rice, naan or yoghurt flatbreads.
notes: Vegan and toddler-friendly — just omit the chilli powder. Freezes very well; make a double batch and freeze half. Lentils thicken as they cool — add a splash of water when reheating.

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high. Add onion and garlic; cook 3 minutes until golden.
Increase heat to high, add mince and break up as it browns.
Add red wine and simmer 1 minute, scraping the pan, until the alcohol smell is gone.
Add the stock cubes, crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves, thyme, salt and pepper. Stir, bring to a simmer.
Reduce to medium and simmer uncovered for 20–30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens. Add a splash of water if it gets too thick.
Remove bay leaves and thyme sprigs. Taste and adjust salt.
Cook spaghetti to packet instructions. Drain, toss with sauce and serve with grated parmesan.
notes: For a richer, slow-cooked version: add ¾ cup water, cover, and simmer on very low for 2–2.5 hours, stirring every 30 min, then uncover for 20 min to thicken. Freezes well.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala

ingredients.name: fresh ginger
ingredients.notes: grated (1 tbsp for marinade, 1 tbsp for sauce)

steps: Mix marinade: yoghurt, 3 garlic cloves, 1 tbsp ginger, garam masala, cumin, coriander, paprika, lemon juice and salt. Add chicken, coat well, and marinate for at least 3 hours (overnight is best).
Sear chicken: heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan over high heat until smoking. Cook chicken in batches, leaving 2 minutes per side to get a char. The inside finishes cooking in the sauce. Set aside.
In the same pan over medium-high, melt butter with oil. Add onion, remaining garlic and ginger with a pinch of salt. Cook 5–7 minutes until golden.
Add paprika, garam masala and turmeric. Cook 2 minutes, stirring.
Add tomato passata and water. Bring to a simmer, cover and cook on low for 15 minutes.
Blend the sauce smooth with a stick blender. Return to the pan.
Stir in cream, sugar and butter. Add the chicken and simmer until cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Serve over basmati rice, garnished with coriander if using.
notes: Marinating overnight makes a big difference to the flavour and texture. The sauce can be made ahead and frozen — add cream and chicken fresh when reheating. Dial back the cayenne (or skip it) for a family-friendly version.

title: Mushroom Risotto
steps: Sauté mushrooms in batches: heat oil and half the butter in a large pot over high heat. Cook half the mushrooms until golden (about 4 min). Add half the garlic, season, cook 1 more minute. Remove to a bowl. Repeat with the rest.
Reduce heat to medium. Melt 1 tbsp butter in the same pot. Add shallots and remaining garlic; cook 2 minutes until soft.
Add wine and simmer 2 minutes, scraping the base, until mostly evaporated.
Add the rice and stir for 1 minute until the edges turn translucent.

Add stock one ladleful at a time (about 1.5 cups per addition), stirring and waiting for it to absorb before adding the next. Continue for about 20 minutes until the rice is cooked but still has a little bite and the risotto is creamy.
Stir in the cream and remaining butter vigorously. Stir in the parmesan. Season well.
Fold in most of the mushrooms, reserving some for topping. Serve immediately, topped with reserved mushrooms and extra parmesan.
notes: Risotto must be served immediately — it thickens as it sits. Keep the stock warm in a separate pan so it doesn't stop the cooking when added. Use a mix of mushrooms (chestnut, shiitake, portobello) for more depth.

title: Thai Green Curry

ingredients.name: Thai green curry paste
ingredients.notes: Maesri recommended; use 3 tbsp for milder

steps: Heat oil in a wide pan over medium-high. Add curry paste, garlic, ginger and lemongrass. Fry 2–3 minutes until fragrant and mostly dried out.
Add stock and coconut milk, stir to dissolve the paste.
Add fish sauce, sugar and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a simmer.
Add chicken, reduce heat to medium and cook for 7 minutes.
Add vegetables and cook a further 3–5 minutes until just tender.
Taste and adjust fish sauce and sugar. Stir in lime juice.
Serve over jasmine rice, topped with fresh basil or coriander.
notes: Maesri paste (small tins, Asian supermarkets) gives the best flavour. For a toddler-friendly version, use only 2 tbsp paste and skip extra chilli. Kaffir lime leaves can be found dried in most supermarkets. Swap chicken for prawns — add them at step 5 and cook 3 minutes.

title: Chicken Fajitas

ingredients.name: chicken thigh
ingredients.notes: skinless, boneless — or 2 large chicken breasts, halved horizontally

steps: Mix lime juice, orange juice, olive oil, garlic, cumin, salt and pepper in a bowl. Add chicken, coat well. Marinate for at least 1 hour (up to 24 hours, or freeze and defrost overnight).
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over high heat until smoking. Cook peppers and onion in batches in a single layer — char for 45 seconds, toss with salt and pepper, repeat twice. Remove to a platter.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in the skillet over medium-high. Cook chicken 3–4 minutes per side until caramelised. Rest 3 minutes loosely covered with foil, then slice.
Warm tortillas. Serve chicken and peppers with tortillas, sliced avocado, soured cream and lime.
notes: The citrus marinade is key — at least 1 hour but overnight is better. High heat and not moving the veg is what gives the char. Can sub beef strips or white fish.

title: Yakisoba (Japanese Stir-Fried Noodles)
steps: Mix all sauce ingredients together in a small bowl and set aside.
Cut protein into bite-sized pieces. Prep all vegetables.
Heat oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the protein and cook until browned.
Add onion, carrot and pepper; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add cabbage and bean sprouts and cook until starting to wilt.
Push everything to the sides of the wok. Add noodles to the centre, pressing them under the vegetables. Cover and lower heat for 2 minutes to steam and soften the noodles.
Stir everything together to combine. Pour in the sauce and toss well over medium heat for 1–2 minutes until the noodles are coated and glossy.
Season with salt and pepper if needed. Serve topped with aonori and pickled ginger.
notes: Tonkatsu sauce is available in most Asian supermarkets (Bull-Dog brand is common). In a pinch, use extra Worcestershire with a dash of ketchup. Yakisoba noodles are sold pre-steamed in vacuum packs — use fresh rather than dried for best results.
